PCAC.description: Power Cost Adjustment Clause — applies to all non-PCAC2 metered rates

variables.A: Power cost adjustment rate ($/kWh), rounded to 4 decimal places
variables.C: Non-PCAC2 cost of power purchased in the most recent month ($). Recorded in Acct 555 (Class A&B) or Acct 545 (Class C)
variables.S: Total non-PCAC2 kWh sold in the most recent month
variables.U: Base cost of power = $0.0792/kWh (constant until changed by PSCW)

PCAC.notificationTrigger: If monthly adjustment exceeds $0.0150/kWh more than 3 times in any 12-month rolling period, utility must notify PSCW

PCAC2.description: Power Cost Adjustment Clause 2 — applies exclusively to Cp-4 tariff

variables.DCA: Demand-related adjustment rate ($/kW), rounded to 3 decimal places, applied to on-peak billing demand
variables.WDC: Current demand-related cost of power purchased/produced for Cp-4 customers ($). Includes facilities charge and demand-related charges.
variables.RBD: Retail on-peak billing demands of Cp-4 customers in the most recent month (kW)

variables.ECA: Energy-related adjustment rate ($/kWh), rounded to 4 decimal places, applied to all Cp-4 metered sales
variables.WEC: Current energy-related cost of power purchased/produced for Cp-4 customers ($). Includes energy-related cost components and reactive energy charges.
variables.RE: Total kWh sold to Cp-4 customers in the most recent month
variables.BECF: Base Energy Cost Factor = $0.0432/kWh (constant until changed by PSCW)

Rg-1.name: Residential Service
Rg-1.applicability: Residential single-phase and three-phase customers for ordinary household purposes. Single-phase motors ≤5 HP individual rated capacity (utility permission required above).
Rg-1.latePaymentCharge: 1% per month on unpaid balance if not paid within 20 days of issuance

Rg-2.name: Residential Service – Optional Time of Day
Rg-2.applicability: Optional to all Rg-1 customers. Must apply for service. Minimum 1-year commitment once enrolled. Waives right to billing adjustments if another schedule would be lower.

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

Rg-2.annualReview: Utility reviews billing annually per Wis. Admin. Code ch. PSC 113
Rg-2.movingProvision: If customer moves within service territory, both original and new customer may retain TOD billing or transfer to Rg-1 at no cost
Rg-2.jointResidentialCommercial: Billed on a rate determined by customer load

Gs-1.name: General Service
Gs-1.applicability: Single and three-phase customers: commercial, institutional, government, farm, and other. Monthly Maximum Measured Demand must not exceed 50 kW for 3 or more months in a consecutive 12-month period.
Gs-1.demandDetermination: Maximum Measured Demand = average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption. Meter reset monthly.
Gs-1.farmCustomerDefinition: Person/org using electric service for individual farm operation or residential use on farm. Farm = tract ≥3 acres used for agricultural/dairy/livestock/poultry/floriculture, or where principal income derives from farm.

Gs-2.name: General Service – Optional Time of Day
Gs-2.applicability: Optional to all Gs-1 customers. Must apply. Minimum 1-year commitment. Waives right to billing adjustments if another schedule would be lower.

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

Gs-2.movingProvision: Customer may retain TOD billing or transfer to Gs-1 at no cost
Gs-2.jointResidentialCommercial: Billed on a rate determined by customer load
Gs-2.annualReview: Utility reviews billing annually per Wis. Admin. Code ch. PSC 113

Cp-1.name: Small Power Service
Cp-1.applicability: All customer types. Monthly Maximum Measured Demand >50 kW and ≤200 kW for 3 or more months in a consecutive 12-month period.
Cp-1.exitThreshold: Remains on Cp-1 until demand <50 kW for 12 consecutive months
Cp-1.energyLimiterLogic: Each month, customer is billed the LESSER of: (1) Energy Limiter rate × total kWh, OR (2) Energy Charge + Demand Charge combined. Customer charge, distribution demand charge, and PCAC are always billed regardless.

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

transformerOwnership.notes: Customer owns and maintains own transformer/substation. Equipment subject to utility inspection and approval.

discounts.applicationSequence: Primary metering discount applied first, then transformer ownership credit

demandDetermination.maximumMeasuredDemand: Average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption. Meter reset monthly.
demandDetermination.distributionDemand: Highest monthly Maximum Measured Demand in current month or preceding 11-month period (12-month rolling peak)
demandDetermination.onPeakBilledDemand: On-peak Maximum Measured Demand in the current month

Cp-2.name: Large Power Time of Day Service
Cp-2.applicability: All customer types. Monthly Maximum Measured Demand >200 kW and ≤1,000 kW for 3 or more months in a consecutive 12-month period.
Cp-2.exitThreshold: Remains on Cp-2 until demand <200 kW for 12 consecutive months

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

transformerOwnership.notes: Customer owns and maintains own transformer/substation. Subject to utility inspection.

discounts.applicationSequence: Primary metering discount applied first, then transformer ownership credit

demandDetermination.maximumMeasuredDemand: Average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption. Meter reset monthly.
demandDetermination.distributionDemand: Highest monthly Maximum Measured Demand in current month or preceding 11-month period (12-month rolling peak)
demandDetermination.onPeakBilledDemand: On-peak Maximum Measured Demand in the current month

Cp-3.name: Industrial Power Time-of-Day Service
Cp-3.applicability: All customer types. Monthly Maximum Measured Demand >1,000 kW and ≤5,000 kW for 3 or more months in a consecutive 12-month period.
Cp-3.exitThreshold: Remains on Cp-3 until demand <1,000 kW for 12 consecutive months

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

transformerOwnership.notes: Customer owns and maintains own transformer/substation. Subject to utility inspection.

discounts.applicationSequence: Primary metering discount applied first, then transformer ownership credit

demandDetermination.maximumMeasuredDemand: Average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption. Meter reset monthly.
demandDetermination.distributionDemand: Highest monthly Maximum Measured Demand in current month or preceding 11-month period (12-month rolling peak)
demandDetermination.onPeakBilledDemand: On-peak Maximum Measured Demand in the current month

Cp-4.name: Large Industrial Power Time-of-Day Service
Cp-4.applicability: All customer types. Monthly Maximum Measured Demand >5,000 kW for 3 or more months in a consecutive 12-month period AND calculated load factor ≥85%.
Cp-4.loadFactorFormula: Load Factor = kWh / (kW × 730), where kW = Maximum Measured Demand and 730 = avg hours/month
Cp-4.exitThreshold: Remains on Cp-4 until demand <5,000 kW for 12 consecutive months

pricingPeriods.off_peak: All other times including all day Saturday, Sunday, and holidays

transformerOwnership.notes: Customer owns and maintains own transformer/substation. Subject to utility inspection.

discounts.applicationSequence: Primary metering discount applied first, then transformer ownership credit

demandDetermination.maximumMeasuredDemand: Average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption. Meter reset monthly.
demandDetermination.distributionDemand: Highest monthly Maximum Measured Demand in current month or preceding 11-month period (12-month rolling peak)
demandDetermination.onPeakBilledDemand: On-peak Maximum Measured Demand in the current month

Cp-5.name: Coincident Demand Metering Rider
Cp-5.applicability: Available to Cp-2, Cp-3, and Cp-4 customers with multiple separately-metered service entrances on contiguous properties whose combined Maximum Coincident Demand exceeds applicable threshold (200 kW for Cp-2, 1,000 kW for Cp-3, 5,000 kW for Cp-4) for 3 or more months in consecutive 12-month period.
Cp-5.customerCharge: Monthly customer charge per each separately metered location as if served individually on respective Cp-2/Cp-3/Cp-4 schedule
Cp-5.distributionDemand: Sum of distribution demands of each separately metered location as if served individually. Utility may bill coincident basis if multiple meters/drops are installed at same premises for utility's convenience.

demandDetermination.maximumMeasuredDemand: Average kWh in 15 consecutive minutes of greatest consumption to a single metered service point. Meter reset monthly.
demandDetermination.maximumCoincidentDemand: Greatest single demand in 15 consecutive minutes during the month resulting from combination of all separately metered services
demandDetermination.maximumCoincidentOnPeakDemand: Maximum Coincident Demand occurring during the on-peak period
demandDetermination.billedDemand: Same as Cp-2/Cp-3/Cp-4 with On-peak Maximum Demand replaced by Maximum Coincident On-Peak Demand

Cp-5.energyCharge: Same as applicable Cp-2, Cp-3, or Cp-4 schedule
Cp-5.minimumBill: Same as applicable Cp-2, Cp-3, or Cp-4 schedule
generalProvisions: Customer class determined by maximum coincident demand
Customer may terminate with 30 days written notice; responsible for any resulting bill increases
Primary metered services may only be combined with other primary metered services
All metering points that can be combined must be combined together
Utility must annually notify all Cp-2, Cp-3, and Cp-4 customers that coincident billing is available upon request

Ms-1.name: Lighting Service
Ms-1.applicability: Lighting service. Closed to new mercury vapor lights.

Ms-3.name: Athletic Field Lighting Service
Ms-3.applicability: Sports and athletic field lighting at primary voltages with ≥40 kW connected load. Usage limited to evening hours after 6:00 PM prevailing time.

NLMP.name: New Load Market Pricing Service
NLMP.applicability: Existing customers with Maximum Measured Demand >200 kW for 3+ months in 12-month period AND expected demand growth ≥400 kW; OR new customers with expected peak demand ≥400 kW. Must state NLMP was a factor in decision to expand/take service.
eligibilityRequirements: Informed utility at least 3 months prior to service
Electric meters recording 15-minute interval data prior to commencement
Energy efficiency assessment completed by Focus on Energy Advisor within 12 months prior or within 6 months after taking service
NLMP.notAvailableTo: Customers transferring existing load from another Wisconsin electric utility
NLMP.termination: Customer may terminate on annual anniversary date with 30 days written notice. Customers removed or terminating may not return to NLMP.

charges.incrementalDemandRate: Pass-through of MISO/WPPI fixed costs including: MISO Resource Adequacy (firm load only), MISO NITS, MISO Schedule items (1,2,10,10-FERC,11,26,33,43), Direct Network Upgrade Charges. Multiplied by distribution loss factor and gross receipts tax factor. Also multiplied by 1.02 for transmission losses.

incrementalEnergyRate.description: Pass-through of hourly MISO energy costs: Day-Ahead LMP at WEC.WPPI node, RSG, Uplift, Ancillary Services, Market Administration. Plus adder of $0.0005/kWh.

incrementalDistributionDemandRate.option1: Distribution Demand above Baseline subject to same charges as below-Baseline demand. Customer receives construction allowance per Electric Rules.
incrementalDistributionDemandRate.option2: Distribution Demand above Baseline NOT subject to Distribution Demand charges. Customer does NOT receive construction allowance.

baselineLogic.existingCustomers: Most recent 12 consecutive months preceding Approved Application date. Includes Monthly On-Peak Baseline Energy Level, Monthly Off-Peak Baseline Energy Level, Monthly Baseline Demand Level. Baseline Distribution Demand = Distribution Demand in month immediately preceding Approved Application.
baselineLogic.newCustomers: Based on customer-supplied forecast. Baseline levels ≥70% of Year 1 forecast. After Year 1, adjusted to reflect % of actual Year 1 consumption.

grossReceiptsTaxFactor.applies: Customers outside municipal boundaries of utility

Pgs-1.name: Parallel Generation (20 kW or less) – Net Energy Billing
Pgs-1.applicability: Single and three-phase customers with generating facilities ≤20 kW total generating capability connected in parallel with utility and approved by utility.
Pgs-1.billing: Monthly net energy basis. Customer pays fixed charge and energy charge per their applicable retail rate. Monthly credits = net excess kWh produced × (applicable energy charge + monthly PCAC).
Pgs-1.creditHandling: Credit balance ≤$25: carried forward to subsequent bills. Credit balance >$25: utility reimburses by check upon request.
Pgs-1.meteringNote: Ratchet and other reverse-prevention devices removed from meter to allow reverse power flow. Customers with TOU metering may request net energy metering installation.
Pgs-1.customerObligation: See Wis. Admin. Code ch. PSC 119

Pgs-2.name: Customer-Owned Generation Systems (Greater than 20 kW)
Pgs-2.applicability: Single and three-phase customers with generating facilities >20 kW connected in parallel with utility. Customers not desiring to sell energy may negotiate a buy-back rate.
Pgs-2.powerFactor: Customer must operate at net power factor ≥90%
Pgs-2.energyPayment: Monthly payments for all electricity delivered to utility at rates per latest wholesale supplier rates (unless not reflecting avoided costs — Commission may set rates on request)
Pgs-2.negotiatedBuyBackRate: Must be ≤full avoided cost. Utility must respond within 30 days of initial/subsequent proposals. Rejection must include counter-offer. Contracts filed with Commission.
Pgs-2.maintenanceRate: Lower demand charges available during scheduled maintenance with advance written utility approval. Demand charges prorated based on authorized maintenance days / days in billing period.
Pgs-2.energySuppliedByUtility: Billed per standard applicable rate schedules

Pgs-3.name: Solar Renewable Energy Distributed Generation (Limited Participation)
Pgs-3.applicability: Customers owning small solar PV facilities approved by utility. Individual project nameplate capacity limited to ≤6 kW dc or total PV capacity available under tariff, whichever is less. Total PV capacity limit: 16 kW dc (extendable to maximum additional 48 kW dc if unsubscribed capacity available under WPPI Schedule for Purchase of Solar PV Energy).
Pgs-3.energyPurchaseRate: Utility purchases 100% of generator output. Buy-back rate = WPPI Energy's wholesale PV energy buy-back rate per WPPI Schedule for Purchase of Solar PV Energy. Customer receives monthly credit.
Pgs-3.contractRequirement: 10-year contract required. WPPI Energy obtains all Renewable Energy Credits and Attributes.
Pgs-3.interconnectionRequirements: Per Wis. Admin. Code ch. PSC 119. Interconnection at service voltage only.
Pgs-3.distributionOutages: No compensation for energy not delivered during distribution outages.

SS.name: Shared Savings (Limited Participation)
SS.applicability: Customers served under Gs-1, Gs-2, Cp-1, Cp-2, Cp-3, and Cp-4 implementing eligible energy efficiency projects. Must meet credit requirements and enter Shared Savings agreement.
SS.programFocus: Eligible projects where advance utility payment ≤$50,000 (principal focus). Case-by-case up to $500,000 for qualifying expansion/new customer projects.
eligibilityRequirements: Completed Shared Savings application approved by utility and WPPI Energy
Projects must reduce electric use and/or demand for duration of repayment period
Projects must meet minimum efficiency requirements aligned with Wisconsin Focus on Energy program
Customer agrees to right of utility to remove equipment at customer's expense in event of default
Submit Certificate of Project Completion upon completion

CTC-1.name: Commitment to Community Rider
CTC-1.legalBasis: 1999 Wisconsin Act 9 and 2005 Wisconsin Act 141; Wis. Stats. §§ 16.957(5) and 196.374(7)
CTC-1.purpose: Low-income assistance (50% of fees) and energy efficiency programs (50% of fees)
CTC-1.overallCap: Lesser of 3% of all other billed electric charges or $750 per month
CTC-1.taxTreatment: Not subject to Gross Receipts or Sales Taxes

RER-1.name: Renewable Energy Rider
RER-1.applicability: All customers on all rate schedules. Optional.
notes: Renewable energy sold in 300 kWh/month blocks only
Block Charge is in addition to regular monthly charges including PCAC
Service may be limited at utility's discretion based on available renewable energy, usage history, and payment history
Aggregate sales allowed only for multiple facilities owned by the same entity
Service effective beginning of next full billing period after sign-up; Block Charge not prorated
If customer uses less than purchased blocks, billed for all purchased blocks
Cancellation effective beginning of next full billing period; not prorated

TE-1.name: Telecom Equipment Service – Unmetered
TE-1.applicability: Local Exchange Carriers (LECs) and WAN/LAN Radio Transceiver customers requiring electric connection to customer-owned or leased equipment. Limited to locations where metered service is not readily available and individual LEC-owned/leased equipment draws less power than can be practically/economically metered.
TE-1.unmeteredUsageEstimation: Monthly billed kWh = maximum hourly energy consumption (kW per manufacturer specs, confirmed by utility) × 730 hours
TE-1.serviceAgreement: Required. Specifies max hourly consumption per device, testing requirements, and customer-specific terms.
keyConditions: Customer must notify utility within 30 days of any equipment additions, subtractions, replacements, or modifications
Failure to notify may result in back-billing up to 2 years and/or service cancellation
All customer equipment must be available for utility testing at least 30 days prior to energization
Testing waived if same brand/model previously tested by utility
Customer waives right to billing adjustment claims on any alternative rate

OC-1.name: Other Charges and Billing Provisions
OC-1.budgetPaymentPlan: Available per Wis. Admin. Code ch. PSC 113. No fixed budget year. Monthly amount = estimated annual bill / 12. Last month includes year-end adjustment.

reconnectionCharge.note: After-hours: $42 minimum plus overtime labor costs, not exceeding $90 total

OC-1.seasonalReconnection: Same reconnection charges plus all minimum bills that would have accrued had customer not temporarily disconnected
